div1.entryFree:
Brawl, subst. 1) a row, squabble: but he is a devil in private b. (== single combat, duel), Tw. III, 4, 259. be gone, good ancient: this will grow to a b. anon, H4B II, 4, 187. we shall much disgrace with four or five most vile and ragged foils, right ill disposed in b. ridiculous, the name of Agincourt, H5 IV Chor. H5 IV Chor. none basely slain in --s, Tit. I, 353. three civil --s, bred of an airy word, Rom. I, 1, 96 (Ff broils). if we meet, we shall not scape a b. III, 1, 3. III, 1, 3 III, 1, 3 put by this barbarous b. Oth. II, 3, 172. Oth. II, 3, 172
2) quarrel, altercation: his sports were hindered by thy --s, Err. V, 77. with thy --s thou hast disturbed our sport, Mids. II, 1, 87. Tw. V, 364. H5 IV, 8, 69. H6A II, 4, 124. Tit. IV, 3, 93.
3) a French dance: LLL III, 9.
